docs.title: 🦿🪐 neon-sim
docs.text:
Drive your Unitree G1 through your actual room (scanned with Polycam) using the same neon-runtime agent that controls the real robot.

Get started → See architecture → Pairs with neon-runtime →

docs.title: What it does
docs.text:
1. You scan your room with Polycam → get a .usdz file
  2. neon-sim preprocesses it (Y-up → Z-up, adds floor, tags colliders)
  3. Spawns the G1 robot in your scanned space
  4. Publishes the same DDS topics as the real robot — so your neon agent doesn't know it's in a simulator

docs.title: Two backends
docs.text:
Backend Platform Strengths 🥇 Isaac Sim Linux (CUDA) / Windows (WSL) Native USDZ, photoreal, Unitree ships official G1 model 🪶 MuJoCo macOS / Linux / Windows Works today, no GPU required, needs OBJ conversion

Both share the same DDS bridge — pick whichever is easier for you.

docs.title: Why this is cool
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    Scan[📱 Polycam scan<br/>your room] --> Conv[convert_polycam.py]
    Conv --> Room[🏠 your room<br/>as USD]
    Room --> Sim[Isaac / MuJoCo]
    G1U[Unitree G1 USD] --> Sim
    Sim --> Bridge[DDS Bridge]
    Bridge -.rt/lowstate.-> Neon[neon-runtime]
    Neon -.rt/api/sport/*.-> Bridge

    style Scan fill:#9b59b6,stroke:#8e44ad,color:#fff
    style Room fill:#e74c3c,stroke:#c0392b,color:#fff
    style Sim fill:#3498db,stroke:#2980b9,color:#fff
    style Neon fill:#f39c12,stroke:#d68910,color:#fff

Test reckless things in sim. Deploy working things to hardware. The same agent does both.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• 🤖 neon-runtime — the agent that runs on your real G1
  • 📋 g1-runtime — hardware reverse-engineering notes
  • 📱 Polycam — 3D scanning app (LiDAR on iPhone Pro, photogrammetry elsewhere)
  • 🧪 unitree_mujoco — official G1 MJCF models

docs.title: The big picture
docs.text:
flowchart TD
    subgraph MacMini["🖥️ Mac Mini"]
        N[neon-runtime agent]
        N -->|DDS pub| SR[rt/api/sport/request]
        LS[rt/lowstate] -->|DDS sub| N
    end

    subgraph Sim["🎮 neon-sim process"]
        Isaac[Isaac Sim / MuJoCo]
        Bridge[DDS Bridge]
        Isaac <-.joint state.-> Bridge
        Isaac <-.articulation commands.-> Bridge
    end

    SR -->|network/lo| Bridge
    Bridge -->|network/lo| LS

    subgraph Room["🏠 Scanned room (USDZ)"]
        Walls[static meshes]
        Floor[floor plane]
    end
    Room --> Isaac

docs.title: Why DDS as the interface?
docs.text:
The real G1 runs sport_mode on its Jetson, which:

  • Publishes rt/lowstate at 500 Hz (joint state + IMU)
  • Publishes rt/bmsstate at 1 Hz (battery)
  • Subscribes to rt/api/sport/request for RPC commands

neon-runtime is the client side of this. It has zero knowledge that there's a sim in the loop. This gives us:

  • ✅ Zero code change in neon-runtime
  • ✅ Bit-identical motion guard, battery guard, safety logic
  • ✅ Switch sim/real with one env var (G1_NETWORK_INTERFACE=lo vs en0)

docs.title: Why two backends?
docs.text:
Isaac Sim is the right answer — native USD, Unitree ships G1 USD, PBR rendering of your scan textures. But it requires:

  • NVIDIA RTX GPU (~8GB+ VRAM)
  • Linux or Windows-WSL
  • 30GB+ of disk for the install

MuJoCo is the pragmatic answer — runs on your Mac Mini today. It:

  • Loads G1 from XMLs shipped with unitree_mujoco
  • Loads your room as a decoration mesh (no PBR, but functional)
  • Shares the same DDS bridge with Isaac

Pick the one that fits your hardware. The code ahead of the backend is shared.

docs.title: What the bridge does
docs.text:
See DDS bridge for details. TL;DR:

  1. Upstream (sim → DDS): reads Isaac/MuJoCo joint state, packs into unitree_hg.LowState_, publishes at 500 Hz
  2. Downstream (DDS → sim): subscribes to rt/api/sport/request, parses FSM IDs and velocities, drives articulation

docs.title: What neon-sim is NOT
docs.text:
• ❌ A reinforcement learning platform (use Isaac Lab for that)
  • ❌ A perfect hardware simulator (motor dynamics are approximate)
  • ❌ A replacement for hardware testing (walking on real floors beats everything)

It's a way to de-risk new behaviors before they meet 30kg of metal.

docs.title: Isaac Sim vs MuJoCo
docs.text: Pick your fighter:

docs.title: Isaac Sim 🥇
docs.text: Use if: you have an NVIDIA GPU (RTX series), running Linux or WSL.

docs.title: Pros
docs.text:
• Native .usdz import — no OBJ conversion
  • PBR rendering with your scan textures
  • Unitree ships an official G1 USD model (in isaacsim.robot.assets)
  • Omniverse bridge supports ROS 2 / DDS natively
  • Reinforcement learning ready (Isaac Lab)

docs.title: Cons
docs.text:
• Requires NVIDIA RTX hardware (not M-series Mac)
  • Heavy install (~30GB)
  • Linux-first (Windows via WSL is flaky)

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
https://developer.nvidia.com/isaac-sim → pick latest 4.x.

Launch:

NEON_SIM_BACKEND=isaac ./scripts/launch_sim.sh assets/rooms/my_room.usdz

docs.title: MuJoCo 🪶
docs.text: Use if: you're on a Mac, or no NVIDIA GPU, or just want something lightweight.

docs.title: Pros
docs.text:
• Runs everywhere (M-series Mac, Linux, Windows)
  • Fast — no GPU needed
  • Unitree ships G1 MJCF models in unitree_mujoco
  • pip install mujoco and you're done (no 30GB download)

docs.title: Cons
docs.text:
• Can't load USDZ directly — must convert to OBJ (done automatically)
  • No PBR; scan textures become plain mesh color
  • No official DDS bridge — we ship our own (same as Isaac path)

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
pip install mujoco usd-core cyclonedds
git clone https://github.com/unitreerobotics/unitree_mujoco ~/unitree_mujoco

Launch:

NEON_SIM_BACKEND=mujoco ./scripts/launch_sim.sh assets/rooms/my_room.usdz

docs.title: Shared: The DDS bridge
docs.text:
Both backends use the same neon_sim/bridge/dds_bridge.py — which means your agent code, neon-runtime, doesn't know or care which one you pick.

Swap between them to taste.

docs.title: Which should I start with?
docs.text:
• Have a Mac, want to ship tonight → MuJoCo
  • Have a Linux gaming PC with RTX → Isaac
  • Want photoreal demos to show off → Isaac
  • Want something that works on an airplane → MuJoCo

docs.title: DDS Bridge
docs.text: The bridge lives in neon_sim/bridge/dds_bridge.py and is shared between Isaac and MuJoCo backends.

docs.title: Topics
docs.text:
Topic Schema Direction Rate rt/lowstate unitree_hg.LowState_ sim→neon 500 Hz rt/bmsstate unitree_hg.BmsState_ sim→neon 1 Hz rt/api/sport/request unitree_api.Request_ neon→sim event rt/api/sport/response unitree_api.Response_ sim→neon reply rt/api/arm/request unitree_api.Request_ neon→sim event rt/api/voice/request unitree_api.Request_ neon→sim event

The bridge currently implements rt/lowstate, rt/bmsstate, and rt/api/sport/request. Arm and voice RPCs are TODO.

docs.title: LocoClient request translation
docs.text:
When neon-runtime calls g1_walk_forward(0.5, 0.3), under the hood it:

  1. Calls LocoClient.Move(0.3, 0, 0) at 20 Hz for some duration
  2. Move() sets api_id=7103 with {"x": 0.3, "y": 0, "z": 0} as JSON
  3. Publishes to rt/api/sport/request

The bridge:

  1. Receives the Request_
  2. Parses api_id + JSON parameter
  3. Calls _apply_velocity(0.3, 0, 0) which sets the articulation's base velocity target
LOCO_API_SET_FSM_ID    = 7101  # Damp, StandUp, Sit, etc.
LOCO_API_SET_STAND_HEIGHT = 7102  # HighStand, LowStand
LOCO_API_SET_VELOCITY  = 7103  # Move

docs.title: FSM translation
docs.text:
LocoClient commands are "black-box" intents — the real sport_mode runs a complex policy internally to execute them. In sim, we approximate:

FSM ID Name Sim action 0 ZeroTorque Release all joint actuators 1 Damp Soft joint hold (robot falls slowly) 3 Sit Interpolate to pre-authored sit pose 200 Start Stand idle 702 Lie2StandUp Play lie→stand animation 706 Squat2StandUp / StandUp2Squat Play squat↔stand animation

For MVP, transitions are instantaneous (snap to target pose). A v2 would add joint-space trajectory interpolation or use a learned policy.

docs.title: Articulation limitation
docs.text:
Real G1's sport_mode is a full bipedal controller — balance, foot placement, CoM tracking, the works. Our bridge doesn't reimplement that; it uses Isaac Sim or MuJoCo's built-in physics and cheats where needed (e.g., direct base velocity for Move()).

This means:

  • ✅ Can test high-level agent logic (when to arm, when to stop, safety gates)
  • ⚠️ Motor-dynamics-level testing requires hardware or a learned sim policy

Future work: integrate unitree_rl_gym policies for realistic walking.

docs.title: Scanning tips
docs.text:
• LiDAR mode (iPhone Pro) beats photogrammetry for indoor scenes
  • Keep walking, don't stand still — Polycam needs parallax
  • Overlap by ~30% when panning
  • Capture ceilings if you want them in sim (usually skip — robot doesn't fly)
  • Close the walk-loop back to your start point for best alignment

docs.title: Export settings
docs.text:
Polycam → your scan → Export → USDZ → High / Textured.

The file is typically 10-50MB. Save to iCloud for easy Mac access.

docs.title: Importing to neon-sim
docs.text:
# Put the scan in assets/rooms/
cp ~/Downloads/my_office.usdz assets/rooms/

# Let the launch script preprocess it
./scripts/launch_sim.sh assets/rooms/my_office.usdz

# Or preprocess manually to inspect
python3 scripts/convert_polycam.py assets/rooms/my_office.usdz

docs.title: What the preprocessor does
docs.text:
1. Extracts USDZ (it's just a zip) and reads the USD stage
  2. Inspects geometry — prints vertex/face counts and bounding box
  3. Converts Y-up → Z-up — Polycam exports Y-up (ARKit/USD convention), Isaac Sim and MuJoCo prefer Z-up for robots
  4. Adds a floor plane at the scan's minimum Z — Polycam often has tilted floors because of accumulated drift; a flat plane underneath guarantees the robot doesn't fall through
  5. Tags meshes as colliders — static triangle-mesh physics
  6. Copies textures alongside the output USD

docs.title: Coordinate gotchas
docs.text:
Polycam places the origin at your scan start. If the robot spawns inside a wall, you have two options:

  1. Edit the USD in Blender/Cursor to re-center
  2. Use --spawn-x --spawn-y --spawn-z flags when launching
python3 -m neon_sim.isaac.stage --room assets/rooms/my_room_sim.usd \
  --spawn-x 2.0 --spawn-y -1.5 --spawn-z 1.0

docs.title: Mesh is too heavy
docs.text:
Polycam exports can hit 500k+ triangles. Physics gets slow above ~100k.

Options: - Blender decimate: Open the USDZ, select all meshes, Modifier → Decimate (Collapse, ratio 0.3) - Polycam decimate: Set export quality to "Medium" or "Low" - Manual collider boxes: Replace the triangle-mesh collider with a few box primitives for walls/furniture. Much faster physics.

docs.title: Privacy note
docs.text: Your scans show your actual space. We gitignore assets/rooms/*.usdz so you don't accidentally commit your apartment layout to public GitHub.

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text: Get from "empty room scan" to "G1 walking in your room" in ~15 minutes.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
macOS (MuJoCo path)Linux + Isaac Sim 
# Python 3.10+
pip install usd-core mujoco cyclonedds numpy

# G1 MJCF model (one-time)
git clone https://github.com/unitreerobotics/unitree_mujoco ~/unitree_mujoco

# Optional: Unitree Python SDK (for full DDS bridge)
cd /tmp
git clone https://github.com/unitreerobotics/unitree_sdk2_python
cd unitree_sdk2_python
pip install -e .

# Isaac Sim install (requires NVIDIA RTX GPU)
# https://developer.nvidia.com/isaac-sim

# Use Isaac's bundled Python
alias python3="/isaac-sim/python.sh"

# Packages
python3 -m pip install usd-core cyclonedds

# G1 USD is shipped with Isaac Sim:
ls /isaac-sim/extscache/isaacsim.robot.assets/data/Robots/Unitree/G1/

docs.title: 1. Scan a room
docs.text:
1. Install Polycam on iPhone Pro / Android
  2. Scan (LiDAR mode if available — much better than photogrammetry)
  3. Export as USDZ (settings: full quality, textured)
  4. AirDrop or iCloud to your Mac

docs.title: 2. Clone neon-sim
docs.text:
git clone git@github.com:cagataycali/neon-sim.git
cd neon-sim
pip install -e .

docs.title: 3. Drop your scan in
docs.text: cp ~/Downloads/my_room.usdz assets/rooms/

docs.title: 4. Launch
docs.text:
./scripts/launch_sim.sh assets/rooms/my_room.usdz

This auto-detects your backend:

  • Has Isaac Sim? → uses it, preprocesses USDZ → USD
  • Otherwise → falls back to MuJoCo, converts USDZ → OBJ

docs.title: 5. Connect neon-runtime
docs.text:
In another terminal:

cd ../neon-runtime
./scripts/start-local.sh

Then in Telegram: /arm, stand up, walk forward 1m...

The agent has no idea it's in sim — it publishes to the same DDS topics that the real robot's sport_mode service listens to. neon-sim plays both sides of that conversation.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Problem Likely cause Fix "G1 USD not found" Missing Isaac Sim robot assets Set ISAAC_ASSETS_PATH or copy to assets/robots/g1.usd "G1 MJCF not found" No unitree_mujoco clone git clone https://github.com/unitreerobotics/unitree_mujoco ~/unitree_mujoco Robot spawns inside a wall Room origin offset Pass --spawn-x 0 --spawn-y 0 --spawn-z 1.0 to stage Robot falls through floor Collider approximation wrong Edit convert_polycam.py → use convex_decomposition DDS bridge "address in use" Old instance running killall python3 or reboot

See Troubleshooting for more.
